| Tool | Description | Parameters (`*` = required) |
|---|---|---|
| `search_codebase` | Search for patterns, symbols, or text across the project codebase. Respects .gitignore. Returns matching file paths and snippets. | `query`*: The pattern or text to search for<br>`filePattern`: Optional glob pattern to limit search (e.g. "*.ts", "src/**/*.py")<br>`caseSensitive`: Whether search is case-sensitive (default: false) |
| `get_project_structure` | Returns an annotated file tree of the project (respects .gitignore, skips node_modules/build/dist). Useful for understanding project layout before making changes. | `depth`: Max directory depth to show (default: 4)<br>`path`: Subdirectory to start from (default: project root) |
| `get_conventions` | Returns the detected coding conventions for this project: naming rules, file structure, testing patterns, forbidden practices. | â€” |
| `get_stack_info` | Returns the complete tech stack inventory: languages, frameworks, key dependencies, build tools, and test setup. | â€” |
| `get_file_summary` | Returns a structured summary of a specific file: key exports, types, functions, and brief description. Token-efficient alternative to reading the full file. | `filePath`*: Path to the file relative to project root |
| `get_env_vars` | Returns all required environment variable names (from .env.example or code). Shows which are set vs. missing. Never returns values. | â€” |
| `get_package_info` | Returns installed package versions and direct dependencies. Useful before suggesting library usage to avoid API mismatch. | `packageName`: Optional: specific package to look up (e.g. "@trpc/server") |
| `get_impact_of_change` | Shows what files are affected when a given file changes. Returns direct importers and all transitively affected files. | `filePath`*: File path relative to project root (e.g. "src/types.ts") |
| `get_dependency_chain` | Shows the full dependency chain for a file: what it imports and what imports it, with export names. | `filePath`*: File path relative to project root (e.g. "src/utils/auth.ts") |
| `check_for_updates` | Checks if the AI OS artifacts installed in this repo are out of date. Returns update instructions when a newer version of AI OS is available. | â€” |
| `get_memory_guidelines` | Returns repository memory rules and memory usage protocol from .github/ai-os/context/memory.md. | â€” |
| `get_repo_memory` | Retrieves persisted repository memory entries from .github/ai-os/memory/memory.jsonl, optionally filtered by query/category. | `query`: Optional full-text query against title/content/tags<br>`category`: Optional category filter (e.g. architecture, conventions, pitfalls)<br>`limit`: Max entries to return (default: 10, max: 50) |
| `remember_repo_fact` | Stores a durable repository memory entry in .github/ai-os/memory/memory.jsonl using dedupe/upsert rules (marks superseded conflicts and avoids duplicate facts). | `title`*: Short memory title<br>`content`*: Durable fact/decision/constraint<br>`category`: Category (e.g. conventions, architecture, build, testing, security)<br>`tags`: Optional comma-separated tags |
| `get_active_plan` | Returns the persisted active session plan from .github/ai-os/memory/session/active-plan.json. Use after context resets to restore goals and avoid drift. | â€” |
| `upsert_active_plan` | Creates or updates the persisted active plan (objective, criteria, current/next step, blockers). This provides durable task state across context resets. | `objective`*: Primary goal for the current task<br>`acceptanceCriteria`*: Success criteria for task completion<br>`status`: Plan status: active, paused, or completed<br>`currentStep`: Current execution step<br>`nextStep`: Next planned action<br>`blockers`: Optional blockers, comma-separated or newline-separated |
| `append_checkpoint` | Appends a progress checkpoint to .github/ai-os/memory/session/checkpoints.jsonl to preserve intent and execution state during long tool-call sequences. | `title`*: Checkpoint title<br>`status`: Checkpoint status: open or closed (default: open)<br>`notes`: Optional checkpoint notes<br>`toolCallCount`: Optional tool call count snapshot at checkpoint time |
| `close_checkpoint` | Closes an existing checkpoint by id in .github/ai-os/memory/session/checkpoints.jsonl. | `checkpointId`*: Checkpoint id returned by append_checkpoint<br>`notes`: Optional closing notes to append |
| `record_failure_pattern` | Records or updates a failure pattern in .github/ai-os/memory/session/failure-ledger.jsonl to prevent repeating the same mistakes. | `tool`*: Tool or subsystem where failure occurred<br>`errorSignature`*: Short normalized error signature<br>`rootCause`*: Suspected or confirmed root cause<br>`attemptedFix`*: Fix that was attempted<br>`outcome`: Result of the fix: unresolved, partial, or resolved<br>`confidence`: Confidence in diagnosis from 0.0 to 1.0 |
| `compact_session_context` | Creates a compact session summary from active plan, open checkpoints, and recent failure patterns to reduce context stuffing and preserve continuity. | â€” |
| `get_session_context` | Returns the compact session context card with MUST-ALWAYS rules, build/test commands, and key file locations. CALL THIS at the start of every new conversation to reload critical context after a session reset. | â€” |
| `get_recommendations` | Returns stack-appropriate recommendations: MCP servers, VS Code extensions, agent skills, and GitHub Copilot Extensions. Useful for setting up a new developer environment. | â€” |
| `suggest_improvements` | Analyzes project structure and memory entries to return architectural and tooling optimization suggestions (e.g. missing env var documentation, undocumented key paths, skills gaps). | â€” |
| `set_watchdog_threshold` | Configures the automatic watchdog checkpoint interval for the current session (default: 8 tool calls). Increase for complex multi-step tasks; decrease for shorter focused work. Range: 1â€“100. | `threshold`*: Number of tool calls between automatic watchdog checkpoints (1â€“100) |
| `reset_session_state` | Clears all session state files (active-plan.json, checkpoints.jsonl, failure-ledger.jsonl, runtime-state.json, compact-context.md) so a new branch or task starts from a clean slate. Durable repo memory (memory.jsonl) is never modified. | â€” |
| `sync_hosted_memory` | Returns guidance and a prompt template for mirroring durable facts from Copilot hosted/in-context memory into .github/ai-os/memory/memory.jsonl. Lists existing entries to prevent duplication. | â€” |
| `get_context_freshness` | Computes a freshness score (0â€“100) for AI OS context artifacts by comparing them against the stored context snapshot. Returns a list of stale artifacts, changed source files, and targeted sync recommendations. Run after structural code changes to detect context drift. | â€” |
| `prune_memory` | Compacts the repository memory file by running full hygiene (near-duplicate detection, TTL enforcement, superseded entry removal) and physically deleting all stale entries. Returns a maintenance summary with counts of removed vs. kept entries. | â€” |
| `detect_drift` | Scans AI OS artifacts (skills, instructions, agents, MCP config, context snapshot) for drift. Reports missing files, unreplaced template placeholders, stale context snapshot (>7 days), broken MCP server paths, agent schema gaps, and skills not listed in instructions. Returns a formatted report; exits non-zero when errors exist. | `verbose`: Include healthy files in output (default: false) |
| `read_file` | Read the content of a file within the project root. Path traversal outside the project root is blocked. Files larger than 32 KB are rejected with a helpful message. | `path`*: Path to the file, relative to the project root (e.g. "src/utils.ts") |
| `list_directory` | List the contents of a directory within the project root. Returns file names with sizes and directory names. Ignores node_modules, dist, .git, and other build artefacts. | `path`: Directory path relative to project root (default: "." = project root) |
| `run_workflow` | Load and display the execution plan for a named agent workflow from .github/ai-os/workflows/. Use dry_run: true to preview the chain without executing. Omit workflow_name to list all available workflows. | `workflow_name`: Workflow filename (e.g. "feature-pipeline.yml"). Omit to list all workflows.<br>`dry_run`: Show chain without executing (default: true) |
| `boost_prompt` | Analyses a user prompt for vagueness and, when the score is â‰Ą 3, returns up to 3 targeted clarifying questions so the intent can be precisely resolved before implementation. Returns vaguenessScore, triggered flag, questions array, and optional skill routing. Works without repo-index (keyword-only fallback). | `prompt`*: The raw user prompt to evaluate for vagueness.<br>`activeFile`: Optional: currently open file path. When provided, booster is bypassed (file-anchored prompts are specific enough). |
| `detect_intent` | Classifies the intent of a user prompt into one of 9 categories (new-feature, bug-fix, refactor, db-change, test-addition, dependency-update, docs-update, config-change, quick-edit). Returns intentType, confidence, affectedDomain, suggestedSkill, and an optional WORKFLOW-FORK clarifying question. Works without repo-index (keyword-only fallback). | `prompt`*: The user prompt to classify. |
| `search_symbols` | Searches the Repository Intelligence Index (repo-index.jsonl) for named symbols (functions, classes, interfaces, types, enums, variables) by name query. Optionally filter by kind (function | class | interface | type | variable | enum | method) or by tag (auth, database, api, testing, ui, etc.). Returns up to 30 matching symbols with file path, line, signature, and tags. Requires `ai-os --index` to have been run first; gracefully returns empty list if no index exists. | `query`*: Symbol name to search for (partial match).<br>`kind`: Optional symbol kind filter: function, class, interface, type, variable, enum, method.<br>`tag`: Optional domain tag filter: auth, database, api, testing, ui, cache, payments, notifications, config, observability, utils, storage, jobs, search, security. |
| `get_file_purpose` | Returns a concise description of what a source file does, its exports, domain tags, size, and language â€” sourced from the Repository Intelligence Index (repo-index.jsonl). Requires `ai-os --index` to have been run first. Returns null if no index or no entry for the given file path exists. | `file_path`*: Relative path to the source file (e.g. "src/auth/middleware.ts"). |
| `validate_spec_coverage` | Reports spec requirement coverage across all spec files in the repo index. Groups requirements by spec file and shows which are annotated with @spec: (implemented) and which are gaps. Requires `ai-os --index` to have run first. | `show_all`: Show all requirements including implemented ones (default: false â€” gaps only). |
| `get_spec_for_file` | Returns the spec requirements (with IDs and titles) that a given source file implements, based on @spec: annotations in the repo index. Requires `ai-os --index` to have run first. | `path`*: Relative path to the source file, e.g. "src/actions/index.ts". |

## Disabled / Opt-in Tools

The following tools are disabled by default and require explicit opt-in.
Enable them by setting `AI_OS_ALLOW_RUN_TOOLS=1` in your environment or
`"allowRunTools": true` in `.github/ai-os/config.json`.

| Tool | Description | Parameters (`*` = required) |
|---|---|---|
| `run_tests` | Run the project test suite (`npm run test` or equivalent). Disabled by default â€” requires AI_OS_ALLOW_RUN_TOOLS=1 env var or "allowRunTools": true in .github/ai-os/config.json. | â€” |
| `run_lint` | Run the project linter (`npm run lint` or equivalent). Disabled by default â€” requires AI_OS_ALLOW_RUN_TOOLS=1 env var or "allowRunTools": true in .github/ai-os/config.json. | â€” |
| `run_build` | Run the project build (`npm run build` or equivalent). Disabled by default â€” requires AI_OS_ALLOW_RUN_TOOLS=1 env var or "allowRunTools": true in .github/ai-os/config.json. | â€” |

## Usage

Call these tools from any Copilot agent or chat session. Tools with a `condition` are only
active when the matching stack dependency is detected in your project.

> Tip: Call `get_session_context` first to reload MUST-ALWAYS rules before invoking other tools.
